text:
GENITOURINARY ONCOLOGY -- FOLLOW-UP VISIT
Patient: Gerald Hutchinson
DOB: [on file]
MRN: [on file]
Attending: [GU Oncology Attending]
Visit type: Established patient, return oncology office visit

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Return visit for follow-up on olaparib therapy initiated approximately 2 months ago for metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC) with germline and somatic BRCA2 biallelic loss. Evaluate PSA response, toxicity, and overall clinical status.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Mr. Hutchinson is a 71-year-old male with a well-characterized history of high-risk locally advanced prostate cancer, originally diagnosed approximately 4 years ago as cT3bN1M0 (AJCC Stage IVA), Gleason 4+5=9 (Grade Group 5), PSA 87.4 at diagnosis. He was treated with definitive intent using combined androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) with leuprolide, abiraterone/prednisone, and concurrent dose-escalated IMRT (78 Gy in 39 fractions to the prostate/seminal vesicles, with elective pelvic nodal coverage to 54 Gy). He achieved an excellent initial biochemical response with PSA nadir of 0.2-0.3 ng/mL.

Approximately 1 year ago, he developed rising PSA on continuous ADT plus abiraterone with confirmed castrate testosterone, meeting criteria for castration-resistant prostate cancer. At that time, germline testing (Myriad myRisk) identified a pathogenic BRCA2 frameshift variant (c.5946delT, p.Ser1982Argfs*22). Somatic testing via Foundation One CDx confirmed biallelic BRCA2 loss with LOH, along with AR amplification (8x), TP53 p.R175H, RB1 deep deletion, PTEN homozygous deletion, and elevated gLOH at 18%. MSI-stable, TMB low. PSMA PET/CT (68Ga-PSMA-11) at time of CRPC declaration showed a PSMA-avid right iliac chain node (1.1 cm, SUVmax 4.2) and a right posterior iliac crest osseous lesion (SUVmax 3.8), confirming low-volume metastatic disease by PSMA PET.

Given AR amplification (potentially predicting abiraterone-class resistance), he was initiated on enzalutamide 160 mg daily as the next AR-signaling inhibitor. He had an initial PSA response (nadir 0.7 ng/mL at 6 months) but then developed rapid biochemical and radiographic progression at 9 months, with new bone metastases at T10, right posterior 6th rib, left iliac wing, and worsening mid-back pain. MRI spine confirmed T10 involvement without epidural extension or cord compromise. He was referred to radiation oncology and received palliative EBRT to T10 (30 Gy/10 fractions), with good pain response.

In parallel, given confirmed biallelic BRCA2 loss, the decision was made to initiate olaparib (Lynparza) 300 mg orally twice daily per FDA label for mCRPC. Baseline hemoglobin was 11.8 g/dL, platelets 198,000, creatinine 1.1 (eGFR ~68). Denosumab 120 mg SQ every 4 weeks was started concurrently for bone metastasis management, along with calcium and vitamin D supplementation. Olaparib was delayed briefly by 2 weeks during palliative radiotherapy to minimize overlapping hematologic effects.

Today he presents for his 2-month follow-up on olaparib. He notes meaningful improvement in his mid-back pain since completing the T10 radiation -- he rates it 1-2/10 currently versus 4-5/10 prior to radiation and olaparib. He is taking oxycodone 5 mg as needed for breakthrough pain and has used it only 2-3 times in the past week. He does, however, endorse increased generalized fatigue, which he describes as more profound than his fatigue on enzalutamide. He also notes mild nausea, primarily in the morning and occasionally around the time of his evening olaparib dose. He has been managing this with ginger tea and small frequent meals. He denies vomiting. No dyspnea at rest or with moderate exertion. No lightheadedness or presyncope. No palpitations. No new bone pain beyond the pre-existing, improving T10 site. No headache, no confusion, no visual changes. Appetite is mildly reduced. He has not had a fall. No urinary urgency exacerbation. Bowel habits normal. Wife accompanies today and confirms his functional status is largely maintained at home.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
Positive: Fatigue (grade 1-2, increased from prior baseline), nausea without vomiting (grade 1, intermittent), mild back pain (improving, grade 1), mild reduction in appetite.
Negative: No dyspnea, no lightheadedness, no hemoptysis, no hematuria, no hematochezia, no melena, no chest pain, no fever, no chills, no night sweats, no significant weight loss, no new neurological symptoms, no lower extremity edema or weakness.

---

MEDICATIONS (Current)
- Olaparib (Lynparza) 300 mg PO BID (initiated ~2 months ago)
- Leuprolide acetate 22.5 mg IM q3 months (continuing for castration maintenance)
- Denosumab (Xgeva) 120 mg SQ q4 weeks (started at time of olaparib initiation)
- Prednisone 5 mg PO BID (carried over from abiraterone era; currently being tapered -- down to 5 mg daily per last visit plan)
- Oxycodone 5 mg PO q6h PRN breakthrough pain
- Docusate 100 mg PO BID
- Metformin 1000 mg PO BID (type 2 diabetes)
- Lisinopril 10 mg PO daily (hypertension)
- Amlodipine 5 mg PO daily (hypertension)
- Calcium carbonate 1250 mg PO BID + Vitamin D3 1000 IU daily (with denosumab)
- Ondansetron 4 mg PO q8h PRN nausea (newly prescribed at last visit; patient using sparingly)
- Low-dose aspirin 81 mg PO daily

ALLERGIES: No known drug allergies.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
Vital signs: BP 128/76 mmHg, HR 72 bpm (regular), RR 16, Temp 37.0°C, SpO2 97% on room air, Weight 179 lbs (down approximately 3 lbs from 2 months ago).
General: Well-appearing, mildly fatigued-looking, in no acute distress. Alert and oriented x3. Conversational and engaged.
HEENT: Anicteric sclerae. Conjunctivae mildly pale. Oropharynx clear, no mucositis.
Neck: No cervical or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No peripheral edema.
Pulmonary: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheeze, crackle, or rhonchi.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. Liver edge not palpable. No splenomegaly. Normal bowel sounds. No right upper quadrant tenderness.
Musculoskeletal: Mild tenderness to palpation over the mid-thoracic spine at T9-T11, improved compared with last visit. No new point tenderness over ribs or pelvis. Gait intact.
Neurological: No focal motor or sensory deficits. Cranial nerves grossly intact. No signs of cord compromise.
Lymph nodes: No inguinal, femoral, axillary, or cervical lymphadenopathy noted on exam.
Skin: No rash, no petechiae, no ecchymosis.
Rectal: Deferred (patient had full prostate treatment; DRE not performed today).

---

LABORATORY RESULTS (Today)
- PSA: 1.1 ng/mL (prior: 2.8 ng/mL at olaparib initiation, 2.3 ng/mL at enzalutamide discontinuation)
  --> 61% PSA decline from olaparib baseline, consistent with meaningful biochemical response
- Testosterone: 8 ng/dL (confirmed castrate)
- CBC:
  - Hemoglobin: 10.4 g/dL (prior: 11.8 g/dL at olaparib initiation; prior to that, 11.2 g/dL on enzalutamide)
  - Hematocrit: 31.8%
  - MCV: 88 fL (normocytic)
  - WBC: 5,200/mcL (differential not flagged)
  - Platelets: 182,000/mcL
  - Reticulocyte count: 1.2% (mildly low-normal, reticulocyte index ~0.9 -- borderline inadequate response)
- Comprehensive metabolic panel:
  - Creatinine: 1.1 mg/dL, eGFR ~68 mL/min/1.73m2 (stable)
  - ALT: 28 U/L, AST: 31 U/L (normal)
  - Alkaline phosphatase: 112 U/L (mildly elevated, consistent with known osseous disease; stable from prior)
  - Total bilirubin: 0.7 mg/dL
  - Albumin: 3.6 g/dL
  - Sodium/Potassium/Bicarb: within normal limits
  - Glucose: 148 mg/dL (slightly elevated; consistent with history of diabetes)
  - HbA1c (last checked 3 months ago): 7.1% (reasonable control)
- LDH: 198 U/L (upper limit of normal range; monitoring given hepatic lesion risk with visceral disease)
- Bone alkaline phosphatase: 42 mcg/L (mildly elevated)
- Iron studies: serum iron 62 mcg/dL, TIBC 298 mcg/dL, transferrin saturation 21%, ferritin 105 ng/mL -- consistent with anemia of chronic disease/myelosuppression, not frank iron deficiency
- Vitamin B12 and folate: within normal limits

---

IMAGING RESULTS
No new imaging obtained today. Most recent imaging:
- Bone scan and CT (approximately 1 month ago, at time of enzalutamide discontinuation and olaparib initiation): multifocal bone metastases (T10, right posterior 6th rib, left iliac wing, right posterior iliac crest). No visceral metastases at that time.
- MRI spine (same timeframe): T10 osseous metastasis with marrow replacement, no epidural disease, no cord compromise.
- PSMA PET/CT is planned at 6 months on therapy for comprehensive restaging.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

Mr. Hutchinson is a 71-year-old male with mCRPC harboring germline and somatic biallelic BRCA2 loss (confirmed by germline testing and Foundation One CDx with LOH), now 2 months into treatment with olaparib 300 mg BID. He is demonstrating a strong and gratifying PSA response, with a 61% decline from 2.8 ng/mL to 1.1 ng/mL -- this degree of early PSA fall is highly consistent with on-target PARP inhibitor activity in a BRCA2-deficient tumor and tracks well with what we observe in the PROfound trial cohort.

The primary concern today is the declining hemoglobin: from 11.8 g/dL at baseline to 10.4 g/dL now, representing a 1.4 g/dL drop over 8 weeks. The anemia is normocytic, with a borderline reticulocyte index suggesting a hypoproliferative pattern rather than hemolysis or blood loss. Iron studies do not support iron deficiency as a primary driver. This picture is most consistent with early olaparib-related marrow suppression (erythroid toxicity is a well-described class effect of PARP inhibitors, occurring in roughly 20-25% of patients on olaparib and sometimes necessitating dose reduction or transfusion). At this point, the hemoglobin is 10.4 g/dL -- he is not acutely symptomatic from anemia (no dyspnea, no lightheadedness), so I will hold on transfusion and dose reduction for now. However, this will require close monitoring as there is a real risk of progression to symptomatic, transfusion-requiring anemia if the marrow suppression worsens.

1. Metastatic CRPC, BRCA2-mutated, on olaparib:
   - PSA response encouraging: 1.1 ng/mL (61% decline). Continue olaparib 300 mg BID.
   - Back pain improved significantly following T10 radiation, consistent with a good palliative response. Analgesic needs are minimal.
   - Continue leuprolide ADT for castration maintenance (next injection due in ~5 weeks -- coordinate with nursing).
   - Continue denosumab 120 mg SQ q4 weeks. Remind patient regarding calcium/vitamin D compliance. Dental evaluation should be up to date (checking records -- patient reports dental visit ~3 months ago, no planned invasive procedures).
   - Restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and bone scan planned at 6 months (approximately 4 months from now). PSMA PET/CT also planned at that timeframe for comprehensive response assessment.

2. Emerging anemia (grade 1-2; hemoglobin 10.4 g/dL):
   - Pattern consistent with olaparib-related erythroid suppression (hypoproliferative, normocytic, adequate ferritin, normal B12/folate).
   - No transfusion indicated today -- patient is asymptomatic with Hgb 10.4.
   - Repeat CBC in 2 weeks. If hemoglobin falls below 9.0 g/dL or patient becomes symptomatic, reconsider dose reduction to 250 mg BID or 200 mg BID per label guidance, and reassess need for transfusion support.
   - Avoid ESAs given active malignancy and thrombotic risk in this setting.
   - Will check erythropoietin level at next visit to guide further decision-making.
   - Counsel patient regarding symptoms to watch for: progressive dyspnea, exertional limitation, lightheadedness -- to call the oncology nurse line or present to ER if these develop.

3. Nausea (grade 1, intermittent):
   - Likely olaparib-related, a very common side effect in the first few months.
   - Continue ondansetron 4 mg PRN. Consider scheduling it with morning olaparib dose if morning nausea is most prominent.
   - Reinforce dietary modifications: take olaparib with food, small frequent meals, avoid large high-fat meals.
   - If nausea worsens to grade 2 or interferes with oral intake, consider adding scheduled metoclopramide or switching to scheduled ondansetron 8 mg BID.

4. Fatigue (grade 1-2):
   - Multifactorial: olaparib myelosuppression, ADT-related fatigue, anemia contributing. No evidence of worsening bone pain or neurological compromise to explain a new functional decline.
   - ECOG PS 1 maintained.
   - Light aerobic activity encouraged as tolerated. Referral to physical therapy for energy conservation strategies placed today.
   - Optimize sleep hygiene and daytime activity schedule.

5. Diabetes/Hypertension:
   - Blood glucose slightly elevated today (148 mg/dL). HbA1c was 7.1% three months ago -- acceptable but bears monitoring. Continue metformin. Will send a repeat HbA1c today.
   - BP well controlled at 128/76 on lisinopril and amlodipine. Continue current regimen.

6. BRCA2 germline variant:
   - Patient was counseled previously regarding cascade testing for first-degree relatives. Check-in today: patient reports his daughter has been tested and is awaiting results, and his son is scheduled for testing. Genetic counseling referral confirmed active.

Return to clinic in 4 weeks (or sooner per CBC results at 2-week check).
Patient and his wife verbalized understanding and agreement with the plan. All questions addressed.

Time spent in counseling and coordination of care: approximately 45 minutes.

[Electronically signed -- GU Oncology Attending]
